title: Fluffy Mustardy Peruvian Peppery Ceviche
description: A light and refreshing Peruvian dish that combines the heat of mustard seeds and the kick of Peruvian peppercorns with the freshness of raw fish. This recipe is a unique twist on traditional ceviche.
instructions: Cut the halibut fillets into small pieces and place them in a shallow dish.
In a small bowl, mix together the Peruvian peppercorns and mustard seeds.
Add the lime juice, aji amarillo peppers, red onion, and cilantro to the bowl and stir to combine.
Pour the marinade over the halibut and toss to coat.
Cover the dish with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Just before serving, season with salt to taste.
Serve chilled, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
To toast the mustard seeds, heat a small skillet over medium heat and add the seeds. Cook, stirring frequently, until fragrant and lightly browned, about 2-3 minutes.
To toast the Peruvian peppercorns, heat a small skillet over medium heat and add the peppercorns. Cook, stirring frequently, until fragrant and lightly browned, about 2-3 minutes.
